name: Summer Vegetable Pickles
recipeIngredient: 3 small zucchini, cut into 5-inch by 1/2-inch batons
10 small red onions with tops, halved
recipeInstructions:
1. In a non-aluminum saucepan, stir together the vinegar, water, salt, peppercorns, garlic, fennel seeds, and sugar.
2. Bring to a boil and cook for 2 minutes.
3. Add fennel and cook until just tender, about 4 minutes. Remove with a spider to a cool plate.
4. Add zucchini and cook until just tender, about 1 minute. Remove to a separate plate.
5. Add cauliflower and cook until tender, about 3 minutes. Remove to a cool plate.
6. Add red onions and cook until tender, about 2 minutes. Remove to a cool plate.
7. Return cooking liquid to a boil and boil for 3 minutes.
8. Remove from heat and stir in oil and vinegar.
9. Divide vegetables among 3 jars (1 or 2 quart jars work best, but any size will do).
10. Cover with the cooking liquid. If there is not enough liquid, add a little white wine vinegar to cover.
11. Allow jars to cool, cover with lids, and refrigerate. Pickles will keep up to a month in the refrigerator.
